commit.message:
Fix game hang by correcting CPU branch timing

Branch taken (+1) and page-crossing (+2) cycle penalties were lost because
branch() incremented this.cycles but execute() overwrote it with the base
cost. Use a branchPenalty field that gets added after execute() returns.
Also remove temporary debug instrumentation from PPU and NesEmulator.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

Add component diagram in draw.io format

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

Fix game hang after screensaver by delivering APU frame counter IRQs to CPU

The APU's isIrqPending() was never checked in the main loop, so games
relying on APU IRQs for timing (e.g. Double Dragon & Battletoads) would
spin indefinitely waiting for an interrupt that never fired.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

Add APU implementation for sound support

Implement NES Audio Processing Unit with pulse, triangle, and noise
channels, wired into the CPU bus and main emulation loop. Audio output
uses javax.sound.sampled at 44100 Hz.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

Fix game freeze after title screen by adding NMI edge detection, PRG RAM, and DMA NMI handling

- Trigger NMI immediately when game enables NMI via PPUCTRL while VBlank is active
- Add 8KB PRG RAM at $6000-$7FFF so game state reads/writes work correctly
- Check for NMI during DMA transfer to avoid missing VBlank interrupts

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

Add README with architecture overview, setup, and API docs

commit.message:
Implement notification system with server cluster, workers, emulators, and load testing

- Notification server: REST API, input validation, template rendering, Redis caching, RabbitMQ routing
- Notification worker: 4 dedicated queue consumers (APNS/FCM/SMS/EMAIL) with retry logic
- Third-party emulator: simulates delivery services with 95% success rate
- Device emulator: receives notifications, WebSocket broadcast, REST stats
- Load client: seeds test data, multi-threaded load test with p95/p99 reporting
- Infrastructure: PostgreSQL, Redis, RabbitMQ, Prometheus, Grafana with pre-provisioned dashboard
- Docker Compose with service health checks and replicas
- 27 unit tests covering validation, services, controllers, consumers
- Component diagram in drawio format

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

commit.message:
fix: correct api test mocks for jsdom environment and flexible header matching

Use jsdom's native window instead of stubbing globalThis, and match headers
with objectContaining to avoid brittleness from additional default headers.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

fix(model-3d): fill triangular gaps in Case 3 split-face extrude

When extruding a split face (Case 3: mixed moved/duplicated vertices),
non_coplanar edges were skipped for wall creation. But when one vertex
of such an edge is moved and the other duplicated, a triangular gap
appears. Now all edges with shifted endpoints get walls, with
deduplication to collapse quads with repeated vertices into triangles.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

fix(model-3d): fix HEMesh corruption from recycled half-edge IDs

When addFace consumed a boundary half-edge, it tried to unlink it from
the boundary chain by following stale next/prev pointers. After ID
recycling via the free list, those pointers could reference completely
different (reused) half-edges. Setting their next/prev to NONE corrupted
face loops, causing faceLoop to crash with "Cannot read properties of
undefined (reading 'next')" on subsequent extrude operations.

Fix: verify the neighbor actually references BACK to the boundary HE
before modifying it. If the ID was recycled, the bidirectional check
fails and we skip the (corrupting) unlink.

Adds regression test for stair-building sequence:
box → split → extrude → split → extrude.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

fix: remove mock data flash on page load

Initialize data arrays as empty instead of mock data so nothing
renders until the real API response arrives.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

Fix checkbox interaction and remove recipe metadata from header

Fixed two UX issues based on user feedback:

1. Checkbox Interaction Fix:
   - Made entire row tappable (not just checkbox icon)
   - Wrapped HStack in Button with PlainButtonStyle
   - Added contentShape(Rectangle()) for full row tap area
   - Users can now tap anywhere on the item to check/uncheck
   - More intuitive and easier to use on mobile

2. Removed Recipe Metadata from Header:
   - Removed recipe name display
   - Removed servings count display
   - Header now shows only:
     - "Shopping List" title
     - Item count ("9 items remaining")
     - Completion message ("All items checked!")
   - Cleaner, more focused UI
   - Aligns with shopping use case (user just needs the list)

Previous behavior:
- Only checkbox icon was tappable (small target)
- Header showed "Green Curry Shrimp" and "4 servings"

New behavior:
- Entire row is tappable (large, easy target)
- Header shows only essential info (item count)
- Simpler, cleaner interface

This improves:
- Touch target size (entire row vs. 20x20px icon)
- User experience on mobile device
- UI clarity and focus
- Matches user's desired simplicity

🤖 Generated with [Claude Code](https://claude.com/claude-code)

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

fix(output): prune summary & post-remove hook display path (#1344)

Two fixes to `wt step prune` output:

**Post-remove hook display path**: when removing a non-current worktree
(the
common prune case), post-remove hooks run at `main_path` but the user
stays at
cwd. Previously `post_hook_display_path(main_path)` was used
unconditionally,
suppressing `@ path` when shell integration is active. Now uses
`pre_hook_display_path(main_path)` when `changed_directory` is false,
correctly
showing the path when the user isn't there.

**Prune summary**: use ampersand pattern for paired worktree + branch
removals,
matching the progress messages' "worktree & branch" phrasing. Unpaired
items
(detached worktrees, orphan branches) list separately.

- `Pruned 1 worktree (with branch)` → `Pruned 1 worktree & branch`
- `Pruned 3 worktrees (with branches)` → `Pruned 3 worktrees & branches`
- `Pruned 1 worktree (with branch), 1 branch` → `Pruned 1 worktree &
branch, 1 branch`
- `Pruned 1 branch, 1 detached worktree` → `Pruned 1 worktree, 1 branch`

> _This was written by Claude Code on behalf of @max-sixty_

---------

Co-authored-by: Claude <noreply@anthropic.com>

Fix feed photos opening zoomed in — suppress tap-zoom for 400ms after opening post

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

feat: use native FlashAttentionScore kernel instead of BMM-Softmax-BMM

Hardware testing proved FlashAttentionScore works on 910ProA with CANN 8.3.
The original crash was a configuration issue, not kernel unavailability.

Changes:
- Enable MS_ENABLE_FLASH_ATTENTION=1 (was disabled)
- Replace BMM-Softmax-BMM patch with thin wrapper around native kernel
- Set use_flash_attention: True in YAML config
- Remove attention.py _repeat_kv patch (native FA handles GQA)

Benefits:
- GQA handled natively (Q=32 heads, K/V=8 heads)
- No 8-dimension ReduceSum gradient issues
- CANN's optimized FA gradient kernel
- Faster and more memory efficient

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

fix: message spacing, thread replies, video upload, delete replies, remove SSO buttons

- Reduce message padding to match Slack (pt-2 pb-0.5 for avatar, py-px for compact)
- Add WebSocket broadcast for channel thread replies so other users see updates in real-time
- Add real-time WebSocket listener in ThreadPanel for channel thread replies
- Add delete button for own replies in thread panel
- Add video file support (mp4, webm, quicktime) to backend allowed MIME types and frontend file inputs
- Add inline video player for video attachments in messages
- Remove non-functional Google/GitHub SSO buttons from login and register pages

Add Donaldson Dinners meal planning app

Personal family meal planner with 4 modes: Monthly Planner (calendar with
weekly themes), Weekly Grocery (stub), Emergency Mode (inventory-aware quick
meals), and Fun Mode (craving search + recipe URL import). Includes freezer,
pantry, and fridge inventory management with add/remove/categorize support.
45+ pre-loaded recipes with smart matching against live inventory.

Stack: React + Vite, TypeScript, Tailwind v4, Zustand (localStorage persist).

commit.message:
Add test runner script for all GUI test suites

Testing/run-all-gui-tests.sh runs all three suites sequentially:
- Smoke test (50 assertions): structure + content validation
- Interaction test (37 assertions): tab click, menu items, states
- Lifecycle test (7 assertions): session tree, isolated launch

Usage: bash Testing/run-all-gui-tests.sh

Total: 94 GUI assertions + 20 unit tests = 114 automated tests

Co-authored-by: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

Clean domain-specific leakage from renderers-ui package

Remove genealogy-specific assumptions from @caii/analysis-renderers to make
it truly generic for other analysis projects:

- Delete 4 domain-specific files: SynthesisRenderer, IdeaEvolutionRenderer,
  TacticCardCell, RelationshipCardCell (~1,487 lines removed)
- Parameterize 11 capture handlers: replace hardcoded source_type:'genealogy'
  with config-driven _captureSourceType (defaults to 'analysis')
- Delete TACTIC_DESCRIPTIONS and STYLE_MAP_CATEGORIES indirection from
  CardGridRenderer; move descriptions to view definition config
- Add apiPathPrefix option to useProseExtraction hook (replaces hardcoded
  /genealogy/ path)
- Rename gen-tactic-* -> ar-card-*, gen-rel-* -> ar-grid-*, gen-card-* ->
  ar-card-* CSS classes across all files
- Remove ~300 lines of orphaned CSS from deleted renderers
- Fix domain language in 5 sub-renderer JSON definitions
- Add grouped_card_list alias for move_repertoire sub-renderer
- Update genealogy_tactics and genealogy_relationship_landscape view defs
  to use template_card, direct category names, and config-driven descriptions
- Track renderers-ui/src/styles/ CSS files

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>
